seo_title: How Mirror-Prox TD Methods Improve Off-Policy Prediction
meta_description: Learn about the behavior-induced Mirror-Prox temporal-difference method, which improves off-policy prediction with a more informative update geometry.
body_html:
Direct answer: The behavior-induced Mirror-Prox temporal-difference method (STHTD-MP) improves off-policy prediction by using a more informative update geometry.

Key Benefits of STHTD-MP

  • Improved convergence rate compared to existing Mirror-Prox TD methods
  • Enhanced stability with a single learning rate for primal and auxiliary variables
  • More accurate predictions using the symmetric part of the behavior-policy Bellman matrix

Comparison with GTD2-MP

The analysis shows that STHTD-MP can have a smaller mean contraction factor than GTD2-MP when the behavior-induced metric improves the saddle-point geometry.

Formal Convergence Analysis

Assumptions
The behavior-induced metric is positive definite, the joint mean system is Hurwitz, and boundedness follows from a Lyapunov argument.

The stochastic recursion converges by the ODE method, providing a formal convergence analysis for fixed-policy linear prediction under standard stochastic approximation assumptions.

source.title: Behavior-Induced Mirror-Prox Temporal-Difference Learning for Faster Off-Policy Prediction
source.summary: STHTD-MP is a new off-policy prediction method that uses a behavior-induced metric to improve the geometry of the temporal-difference update process. The method provides a formal convergence analysis and has been shown to have smaller mean contraction factors than GTD2-MP under certain conditions. It has been demonstrated on several benchmarks, including Random Walk and Boyan Chain.
